outcomes: Develop appropriate listening skills from varied experiences to enrich communication
Demonstrate sustained attention during conversations, stories, and instructions
Respond appropriately to verbal and non-verbal cues in everyday interactions
Express own opinions, needs, ideas, and feelings creatively and confidently
Appreciate the opinions and feelings of others during shared conversations
Distinguish between different environmental sounds to enhance phonological awareness
Identify and match similar sounds in words, chants, and rhymes
Differentiate shapes, sizes, colors, and symbols to build reading readiness
Track objects visually from left to right to establish proper eye movement for reading
Demonstrate correct book orientation by holding a book upright and turning pages from right to left
Show care for books and reading materials by handling them gently
Recognize that print carries meaning and conveys a message
Identify common signs, labels, and logos within their immediate environment
Sort and classify objects according to a single attribute such as color, shape, or size
Match objects in a one-to-one correspondence to build a foundation for counting
Arrange objects in simple patterns or linear sequences based on size
Count sequentially from 1 to 10 using rhymes, songs, and real objects
Demonstrate an awareness of number names in sequence
Identify and name numerals from 1 to 5 in their environment
Map numerals 1 to 5 to their corresponding concrete quantities
Compare objects using terms such as big/small, long/short, or tall/short
Order up to three objects sequentially according to height or length
Identify and name basic external body parts and state their main functions
Demonstrate appreciation for their own body and physical uniqueness
Practice simple personal hygiene routines such as washing hands, brushing teeth, and wiping the nose
Use the toilet or latrine appropriately and wash hands afterwards
Name immediate family members and recognize their roles within the home
Identify key places and people within the school compound
Identify potentially dangerous objects and areas at home and school
Demonstrate standard safety precautions when playing or walking
Perform basic movements including walking, running, jumping, hopping, and skipping safely
Demonstrate body balance, agility, and coordination during group games
Manipulate materials through modeling, tearing, pasting, and threading to develop finger muscles
Demonstrate hand-eye coordination by scribbling or coloring within broad outlines
Sing simple songs and perform rhythmic movements in response to different tempos
Play basic percussion instruments and sound-makers collaboratively
Recognize God as the creator of themselves, other people, and the natural environment
Express gratitude to God through simple short prayers and songs
Handle the Bible with respect as a special book containing God's word
Listen attentively to short biblical stories and extract simple moral lessons
